source_rationale: PRG4 (lubricin/proteoglycan 4) is a marker of synovial fibroblasts and mesenchymal stromal cells in joint tissues
FBN1 (fibrillin-1) is an extracellular matrix gene highly expressed in fibroblasts
PI16 is a well-established marker of universal/adventitial fibroblasts
MFAP5 (microfibril-associated protein 5) is characteristic of matrix-producing fibroblasts
COL1A1 is a canonical fibroblast marker encoding type I collagen
CD248 (endosialin) is a surface marker of perivascular/stromal fibroblasts
CLEC3B (tetranectin) is expressed by fibroblasts and marks a PI16+ universal fibroblast subset
IL1RL1 (ST2/IL-33 receptor) is a hallmark of tissue-resident Th2/ILC2 or regulatory T cells
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for Th2 T cell differentiation
KLRB1 (CD161) marks Th17/tissue-resident memory T cells and innate-like lymphocytes
HPGD is highly expressed in regulatory/Th2 T cells involved in prostaglandin metabolism
IL7R (CD127) is a key marker of memory and helper T cell subsets
CCR4 is a chemokine receptor characteristic of Th2 and regulatory T cells
RORA is a transcription factor associated with Th17/ILC2 lineage commitment
KIT (CD117) marks innate lymphoid cell progenitors and ILC precursors
IL7R is essential for ILC and T cell development and survival
CCR6 marks ILC3 and Th17-like lymphoid cells
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is expressed on activated T cells and ILC progenitors
DLL1 (Delta-like 1) is a Notch ligand involved in lymphoid progenitor differentiation
IL1R1 is expressed on ILC3s and activated innate lymphoid cells
XCL1 (lymphotactin) is produced by activated T cells and NK cells, marking effector lymphocytes
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is a costimulatory receptor on activated T cells
SELL (CD62L) marks naive and central memory T cells
KIT expression along with TNFRSF18 and IL7R suggests thymic or ILC progenitor identity
SPINK2 is a marker of early lymphoid/thymic progenitor cells
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is expressed on activated and regulatory T cells
FABP4 is a lipid-handling gene highly expressed in lipid-laden macrophages (foam cells)
CD68 is a canonical macrophage/monocyte lysosomal marker
APOE is highly expressed in lipid-associated macrophages (LAMs)
SPP1 (osteopontin) marks SPP1+ inflammatory/lipid-associated macrophages
LIPA (lysosomal acid lipase) is characteristic of lipid-processing macrophages
GPNMB is a marker of lipid-associated macrophages in adipose and other tissues
CD36 is a scavenger receptor for lipid uptake in macrophages
GNLY (granulysin) is a cytotoxic effector molecule in NK cells and cytotoxic T cells
GZMB (granzyme B) is a key cytotoxic granule enzyme in NK cells
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK cell receptor marking mature NK cells
FCGR3A (CD16) defines the CD56dim cytotoxic NK cell subset
KLRF1 (NKp80) is a specific activating receptor on NK cells
PRF1 (perforin) is essential for NK cell cytotoxic function
FGFBP2 is a marker of terminally differentiated cytotoxic NK cells
SPON2 is preferentially expressed in CD56dim cytotoxic NK cells
KLRB1 (CD161) is a hallmark of MAIT cells and Th17-like T cells
GZMK marks effector memory T cells with cytotoxic potential
RORA is a transcription factor for type 17 immune responses
CCR6 is a chemokine receptor characteristic of Th17 and MAIT cells
NCR3 (NKp30) expression on T cells is characteristic of MAIT cells
IL7R marks memory T cell populations including MAIT cells
IL1B is a key pro-inflammatory cytokine produced by activated monocytes/macrophages
S100A9 is a canonical marker of inflammatory monocytes and neutrophils
S100A8 forms calprotectin with S100A9, marking inflammatory myeloid cells
FCN1 is a classical monocyte marker
CXCL8 (IL-8) is a neutrophil-recruiting chemokine produced by activated monocytes
LYZ (lysozyme) is highly expressed in monocytes and macrophages
EREG (epiregulin) is produced by activated inflammatory monocytes/macrophages
NLRP3 inflammasome component marks activated inflammatory monocytes
IL7R (CD127) is a key marker of CD4+ helper T cells
CD40LG (CD154) is expressed on activated CD4+ T helper cells
CD2 is a T cell surface marker involved in adhesion and costimulation
MAF is a transcription factor associated with Th2/follicular helper T cell differentiation
CXCR4 is expressed on tissue-homing CD4+ T cells
CD69 marks tissue-resident memory T cells
RGS1 is a marker of tissue-resident T cells
CD8A is the defining marker of CD8+ cytotoxic T lymphocytes
CD8B confirms CD8+ T cell identity as part of the CD8 co-receptor
GZMK marks effector memory CD8+ T cells with intermediate cytotoxicity
CCL5 (RANTES) is highly expressed by activated CD8+ effector memory T cells
CRTAM is expressed on recently activated CD8+ T cells
RUNX3 is a master transcription factor for CD8+ T cell lineage
XCL1 (lymphotactin) is highly produced by activated NK cells
XCL2 is co-expressed with XCL1 in NK cells
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory receptor marking CD56bright NK cells
KLRD1 (CD94) pairs with NKG2A on NK cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein for NK cell activating receptors
FCER1G is a signaling adaptor in NK cell activating receptor complexes
AREG (amphiregulin) is produced by tissue-resident NK cells and ILCs
IGKC (immunoglobulin kappa constant) is a definitive B cell/plasma cell marker
CD79A is a B cell receptor signaling component and canonical B cell marker
MS4A1 (CD20) is a definitive mature B cell surface marker
JCHAIN (J chain) is expressed in antibody-secreting plasma cells
TNFRSF13C (BAFF-R) is a B cell survival receptor
BANK1 is a B cell-specific scaffold protein involved in BCR signaling
CD37 is a tetraspanin predominantly expressed on B cells
S100A9 is a hallmark of inflammatory monocytes and granulocytes
S100A8 with S100A9 forms calprotectin, marking inflammatory myeloid cells
VCAN (versican) is expressed by classical monocytes and inflammatory macrophages
FCN1 is a classical monocyte marker
SERPINB2 (PAI-2) is expressed by activated monocytes/macrophages
AQP9 is preferentially expressed on monocytes and granulocytes
CD93 is expressed on monocytes and early myeloid cells
LST1 is highly expressed in monocytes and macrophages
AIF1 (Iba1) is a canonical macrophage/microglia marker
FCER1G is an Fc receptor signaling component in myeloid cells
MS4A7 marks non-classical/intermediate monocytes and tissue macrophages
SERPINA1 (alpha-1 antitrypsin) is produced by monocytes/macrophages
CTSS (cathepsin S) is highly expressed in antigen-presenting myeloid cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is a key signaling adaptor in myeloid cells
C5AR1 is a complement receptor on monocytes and macrophages
C1QA is a complement component highly expressed by tissue-resident macrophages
C1QB is part of the C1q complement complex marking resident macrophages
LYVE1 is a definitive marker of perivascular/tissue-resident macrophages
MRC1 (CD206) is a mannose receptor marking M2/tissue-resident macrophages
FOLR2 marks tissue-resident macrophages with anti-inflammatory properties
CD163 is a hemoglobin scavenger receptor on tissue-resident macrophages
SELENOP is highly expressed in resident macrophages
RNASE1 is a marker of tissue-resident macrophages
APOD is highly expressed in fibroblasts, particularly reticular/adipose-associated fibroblasts
CXCL14 is a chemokine produced by fibroblasts in skin and other tissues
CXCL12 (SDF-1) is a key chemokine produced by reticular fibroblasts
CFD (adipsin) marks adipocyte-like or reticular fibroblasts
DCN (decorin) is a proteoglycan produced by fibroblasts
LUM (lumican) is an ECM proteoglycan marking fibroblast subsets
GPC3 marks specific fibroblast populations in certain tissue contexts
ADH1B is expressed in adipose-associated/reticular fibroblasts
CTLA4 is a key inhibitory receptor on regulatory T cells (Tregs)
TIGIT is an inhibitory receptor enriched on Tregs and exhausted T cells
ICOS is a costimulatory molecule highly expressed on activated Tregs
BATF is a transcription factor important for Treg and effector T cell function
IKZF2 (Helios) is a transcription factor marking thymic-derived Tregs
RTKN2 is a recently identified marker of regulatory T cells
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is constitutively expressed on Tregs
RGS5 is a canonical pericyte/mural cell marker
ACTA2 (alpha-smooth muscle actin) marks pericytes and smooth muscle cells
TAGLN (transgelin/SM22) is a smooth muscle/pericyte marker
NOTCH3 is a key receptor in pericyte/vascular smooth muscle cell identity
MYL9 (myosin light chain 9) is expressed in contractile pericytes and smooth muscle
MCAM (CD146) is a pericyte surface marker
NDUFA4L2 is upregulated in pericytes under hypoxic conditions
GZMK marks effector memory T/NK cells with cytotoxic capacity
CCL5 is highly expressed by effector memory T cells and NK cells
KLRC1 (NKG2A) marks NK cells and NKT-like cells
GZMA (granzyme A) is expressed by cytotoxic lymphocytes
XCL2 is produced by activated NK and NKT cells
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK cell receptor also found on NKT cells
HLA-DPA1 is an MHC class II gene highly expressed on dendritic cells
HLA-DQA1 MHC class II expression is characteristic of professional antigen-presenting cells
CPVL is a carboxypeptidase enriched in dendritic cells and macrophages
TACSTD2 (TROP2) marks specific dendritic cell subsets
DNASE1L3 is a marker of cDC1 dendritic cells
C1ORF54 is expressed in dendritic cell populations
VCAN marks classical monocytes
FCN1 is a canonical classical monocyte marker
CD163 marks monocytes differentiating toward macrophages
CD14 is the defining marker of classical monocytes
LYZ is highly expressed in monocytes
MS4A6A is expressed on monocytes and macrophages
CYBB (gp91phox) is a NADPH oxidase component in phagocytic myeloid cells
S100A9 marks inflammatory monocytes/granulocytes
S100A8 is co-expressed with S100A9 in inflammatory myeloid cells
VCAN marks classical monocytes
FCN1 is a classical monocyte marker
HSPA1A (HSP70) upregulation suggests stress-activated monocytes
HSPA1B heat shock protein marks stressed/activated myeloid cells
SLC11A1 (NRAMP1) is a phagocyte-specific metal transporter
CD7 is a T/NK cell marker expressed on innate-like lymphocytes
TRGC2 (TCR gamma constant 2) identifies gamma-delta T cells
KLRC2 (NKG2C) is an activating NK receptor also found on gamma-delta T cells
LEF1 is a transcription factor in T cell development
TCF7 marks naive/stem-like T cells and early T cell progenitors
CTSW (cathepsin W) is expressed in cytotoxic lymphocytes including gamma-delta T cells
LINC02446 is associated with innate-like T cell populations
CCR7 is a hallmark of mature/migratory dendritic cells (mregDC/mature DC)
LAMP3 (DC-LAMP) is a specific marker of mature dendritic cells
CCL22 is produced by mature dendritic cells to attract T cells
FSCN1 (fascin) is upregulated in mature/activated dendritic cells
CCL17 is a chemokine produced by mature dendritic cells
BIRC3 is an anti-apoptotic gene upregulated in mature DCs via NF-kB signaling
FCER1A (high-affinity IgE receptor alpha) is a marker of cDC2 dendritic cells
CD1C is a definitive marker of conventional type 2 dendritic cells (cDC2)
CLEC10A (CD301) is a C-type lectin receptor marking cDC2 cells
High HLA-DRA expression is characteristic of professional antigen-presenting DCs
MHC class II expression is a hallmark of dendritic cells
IL1R2 (decoy IL-1 receptor) is expressed on cDC2 and activated myeloid cells
CD8A defines CD8+ cytotoxic T lymphocytes
GZMH (granzyme H) marks terminally differentiated cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
GZMB is a key cytotoxic effector molecule in CD8+ T cells
GNLY (granulysin) is expressed by cytotoxic effector CD8+ T cells
PRF1 (perforin) mediates cytotoxic killing by CD8+ T cells
CD3G confirms T cell receptor complex identity
NKG7 is expressed in cytotoxic lymphocytes
CLDN5 (claudin-5) is a tight junction protein specific to vascular endothelial cells
VWF (von Willebrand factor) is a canonical endothelial cell marker
PECAM1 (CD31) is a definitive endothelial cell surface marker
CAVIN2 is involved in caveolae formation in endothelial cells
SOX17 is a transcription factor essential for arterial endothelial identity
AQP1 is a water channel highly expressed in capillary endothelial cells
EGFL7 is an endothelial-specific secreted factor
GNLY marks cytotoxic innate-like lymphocytes
GZMK marks effector memory lymphocytes
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an NK cell receptor
KLRD1 (CD94) marks NK and NKT cells
CMC1 is expressed in cytotoxic lymphocytes
CD2 is a T/NK cell adhesion molecule
SELL (CD62L) marks circulating/naive lymphocytes suggesting CD56bright NK cells
IL1B is a pro-inflammatory cytokine from activated macrophages
CXCL8 (IL-8) is produced by inflammatory macrophages
CCL20 is produced by activated macrophages to recruit immune cells
IL10 production distinguishes this as a regulatory/resolving macrophage subset
PTGS2 (COX-2) marks inflammatory activated macrophages
EREG is produced by tissue-remodeling macrophages
PLAUR (uPAR) is expressed on activated monocytes/macrophages
KLRB1 (CD161) marks innate-like T cells including ILC2s
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for ILC2 and Th2 cells
IL1RL1 (ST2) is a key ILC2 marker responding to IL-33
AREG (amphiregulin) is a tissue-repair effector molecule of ILC2s
KIT (CD117) marks ILC2 progenitors
IL2RA (CD25) is expressed on activated ILC2s and Tregs
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on activated ILC2s
XCL1 is produced by activated innate-like lymphocytes
IL32 is expressed by activated T cells and NK cells
IL7R marks T cell and ILC populations
ETS1 is a transcription factor important in T and NK cell development
SPOCK2 is expressed in specific T cell subsets
ITGB1 (CD29) marks tissue-resident lymphocytes
